gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: delegateClaim dispatcher applies no caller role check; unprivileged EOA may trigger LP-fee distribution to _tokenBeneficiaries[token]
candidate.claimed_root_cause: Portal.sol delegateClaim applies only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH) and no ROLLER_ROLE or DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE guard before delegatecalling PORTAL_ROLLER
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker triggers LP-fee collection and distribution to a token beneficiary without holding any required role, extracting rewards beyond entitlement

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_ROLLER implementation address is non-zero
live_preconditions.observed_value: not directly readable from live_context.json; PORTAL_ROLLER is an immutable set at construction

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_ROLLER implementation source omits caller role check on delegateClaim handler
live_preconditions.observed_value: unverifiable — PortalRoller source is absent from this repository

live_preconditions.name: At least one token has accrued LP fees and a non-zero _tokenBeneficiaries entry
live_preconditions.observed_value: not enumerated in live_context.json; must be derived from BeneficiaryClaimed / V4LPFeesCollected events

live_preconditions.name: Protocol global switch bit flag is set (protocol is live)
live_preconditions.observed_value: implied live by 1361 BNB balance and active trading, but not directly confirmed

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Dispatcher applies only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH); no ROLLER_ROLE or DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE guard present before delegatecalling PORTAL_ROLLER
Executes raw delegatecall to PORTAL_ROLLER preserving msg.sender; if roller has no caller check the full claim flow runs under attacker's msg.sender
Documents intended invariant 'Only the roller or default admin can call this function' — no on-chain enforcement exists at the Portal dispatcher layer

attacker_path.actor: arbitrary unprivileged EOA holding no protocol role
attacker_inputs: token — address of any launched token with accrued LP fees and a non-zero _tokenBeneficiaries entry
call_sequence: 1. Attacker calls Portal.delegateClaim(token) from arbitrary EOA
3. _delegateToImpl(PORTAL_ROLLER) executes delegatecall into roller implementation with attacker as msg.sender
4. If roller omits caller role check: full LP-fee collection executes, fees sent to _tokenBeneficiaries[token]
state_before: Token has status DEX with accrued uncollected LP fees
_tokenBeneficiaries[token] is set to a known address
Attacker holds no ROLLER_ROLE or DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE
state_after: LP fees collected and transferred to _tokenBeneficiaries[token]
Attacker triggered the distribution without authorization

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: LP fees accrued in DEX pool positions associated with launched tokens
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Attacker does not directly receive funds; attacker triggers premature or unauthorized distribution of LP fees to the beneficiary — constitutes unauthorized reward extraction if the beneficiary is attacker-controlled or if the timing/trigger is restricted
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol loses exclusive control over when and by whom LP fees are claimed; if beneficiary is attacker-controlled the attacker receives LP fees without holding the required role
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: NatSpec invariant restricts delegateClaim to roller or default admin; an unprivileged EOA has no entitlement to trigger this path; if attacker is also the beneficiary they receive LP fees on demand without satisfying the role requirement

setup: Fork BSC at block 108382650 against proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
Fetch PORTAL_ROLLER source from BSCScan to confirm presence or absence of caller role check
Identify a token with DEX status, accrued LP fees, and a known _tokenBeneficiaries entry from BeneficiaryClaimed events
Record beneficiary token and BNB balance before call
expected_assertions: If roller enforces role: call reverts with access control error — REJECT finding
If roller does NOT enforce role: call succeeds, beneficiary balance increases, attacker triggered unauthorized fee distribution — escalate to HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: Call reverts due to role check inside PORTAL_ROLLER implementation — finding is invalid because the invariant is enforced at the implementation layer even though absent at the dispatcher layer
